extraction.one_line_summary: WOODS Michael caught JORGENSON Matteo in final meters of Puy de Dôme while POGAČAR Tadej attacked late, gained 8 seconds on VINGEGAARD Jonas who visibly suffered
tactical_patterns: POGAČAR made vicious acceleration with 1km left on steep Puy de Dôme gradients, forcing VINGEGAARD into unsustainable tempo changes
VINGEGAARD deliberately refused to match POGAČAR's accelerations, climbing at steady tempo instead to minimize oxygen debt on extreme gradients
VINGEGAARD looked back for SEUSS mid-climb after losing POGAČAR's wheel, suggesting team support exhausted earlier than planned

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: Showed more explosive power on steep gradients than VINGEGAARD, needing only 25 seconds but demonstrating superior punch
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: negative: First time grimacing visibly on camera this Tour, unable to match accelerations he typically handles, defensive tactics indicate reduced margin
rider_signals.WOODS Michael: positive: 36-year-old caught younger JORGENSON from behind in final meters, exceptional late-climb surge suggests peak form timing
rider_signals.JORGENSON Matteo: neutral: Led into final meters but caught, strong breakaway riding but lacked finishing punch when caught
rider_signals.SEUSS Christopher: negative: VINGEGAARD actively looked for him when dropped, suggesting domestique unavailable when leader needed support most
